ogTitle: Pillar Valley vs Pilot Wilderness: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Pillar Valley and Pilot Wilderness.
heading: Pillar Valley vs Pilot Wilderness — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Pillar Valley, New South Wales and Pilot Wilderness, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Pilot Wilderness has a lower overall climate risk score (39/100) compared to Pillar Valley (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Pillar Valley or Pilot Wilderness safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Pillar Valley has a flood risk score of 26/100, while Pilot Wilderness scores 36/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Pillar Valley scores 60/100 for bushfire risk. Pilot Wilderness scores 24/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Pillar Valley or Pilot Wilderness?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Pilot Wilderness has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.93% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
